ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Soil Scientists/Conservationists analyze and develop strategies to conserve and improve soil health, ensuring sustainable agricultural practices and environmental protection.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: They conduct research, collect and analyze data, and develop policies to promote soil conservation, fertility, and overall ecosystem health. Their work involves collaborating with farmers, policymakers, and other stakeholders to implement effective soil management practices.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: Master's degree in Soil Science or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in pursuing a career in soil science and conservation can start by taking courses in biology, chemistry, and mathematics.
target_degrees: B.Sc in Agriculture or related field

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can bridge their skills by pursuing certifications or courses in soil science and conservation.

future_builder.description: Soil Scientists/Conservationists with experience in the field can advance their careers by developing expertise in specialized areas, such as soil fertility, erosion control, or policy development.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: At least 2 years of experience as a Junior Soil Scientist or Conservationist

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into soil science and conservation by developing relevant skills and building a portfolio of projects.

roadmap.title: Foundations in Soil Science
resources_keywords: Soil Science Society of India publications

roadmap.title: Capstone and Portfolio Development
topics: Develop a soil conservation project proposal
Create a soil fertility improvement plan
